sourceBook.title: Tas-heelud Du'aa (Duaa made easy)
sourceBook.note: This book is organised by grade (Grades 1-10). Only the Grade Five duas are included here; other grades can be added later as their own files.

contentModel: Each dua has parts[] with arabic, transliteration, translation, and source. Quizzed by selection (no Arabic typing).
reviewNote: Islamic content must be reviewed by a parent/teacher before use. In particular, verify the Arabic text and diacritics (tashkeel) against the book; duas flagged needsReview have longer Arabic.

lessons.title: Du'aas when entering the masjid
lessons.summary: When we enter the masjid we send salutations on Nabi ﷺ and ask Allah to open the doors of His mercy for us.

parts.arabic: بِسْمِ اللهِ، اَللٰهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلٰى مُحَمَّدٍ
parts.translation: In the name of Allaah; O Allah send salutations upon Muhammad ﷺ.
O Allaah, open for me the doors of Your mercy.

questions.prompt: Which dua do we read when entering the masjid?
options: Allaahummaf-tah lee abwaaba rahmatik (O Allah, open for me the doors of Your mercy)
Allaahumma innee as-aluka min fadlik (O Allah, I seek Your bounty)
Allaahumma laka sumtu (O Allah, I fasted for You)
Allaahumma baarik lanaa feehi (O Allah, bless it for us)
questions.correctAnswer: Allaahummaf-tah lee abwaaba rahmatik (O Allah, open for me the doors of Your mercy)
questions.explanation: On entering the masjid we ask Allah to open the doors of His mercy.
Rahmah means mercy — we ask Allah to open its doors when we enter the masjid.

flashcards.front: Entering the masjid — what do we ask Allah for?
flashcards.back: Allaahummaf-tah lee abwaaba rahmatik — 'O Allah, open for me the doors of Your mercy.'

flashcards.front: What does 'Allaahummaf-tah lee abwaaba rahmatik' mean?
flashcards.back: O Allah, open for me the doors of Your mercy.

lessons.title: Du'aas when leaving the masjid
lessons.summary: When we leave the masjid we send salutations on Nabi ﷺ and ask Allah for His bounty (fadl).

parts.arabic: بِسْمِ اللهِ، اَللٰهُمَّ صَلِّ عَلٰى مُحَمَّدٍ
parts.translation: In the name of Allaah; O Allah send salutations upon Muhammad ﷺ.
O Allaah, verily, I seek from You Your bounty.

questions.prompt: When leaving the masjid, what do we ask Allah for?
questions.explanation: On leaving the masjid we read 'Allaahumma innee as-aluka min fadlik' — asking for Allah's bounty.

questions.prompt: When entering the masjid we ask for Allah's mercy, and when leaving we ask for His bounty.

flashcards.front: Leaving the masjid — what do we say?
flashcards.back: Allaahumma innee as-aluka min fadlik — 'O Allah, I seek from You Your bounty.'

flashcards.front: Entering vs leaving the masjid — what do we ask for?
flashcards.back: Entering: the doors of His mercy. Leaving: His bounty.

lessons.title: Du'aa when feeling bodily pain or physical distress
lessons.occasion: Recite when feeling pain or distress in the body.
lessons.summary: When something hurts, we seek protection in Allah and His might from the evil of what we feel and fear.

parts.arabic: أَعُوْذُ بِاللهِ وَقُدْرَتِهِ مِنْ شَرِّ مَا أَجِدُ وَأُحَاذِرُ
parts.transliteration: Aʻoozu billaahi wa qudratihi min sharri maa ajidu wa uhaaziru
parts.translation: I seek protection in Allaah and His might from the evil of what I feel and fear.

questions.prompt: Which dua do we read when feeling pain or distress in the body?
options: Aʻoozu billaahi wa qudratihi... (I seek protection in Allah and His might...)
Allaahumma baarik lanaa feehi... (O Allah, bless it for us...)
Bisawmi ghadin nawaytu (I am fasting tomorrow)
Allaahumma laka sumtu... (O Allah, I fasted for You...)
questions.correctAnswer: Aʻoozu billaahi wa qudratihi... (I seek protection in Allah and His might...)
questions.explanation: When in pain we seek protection in Allah and His might from the evil of what we feel and fear.

flashcards.front: Du'a when feeling pain in the body?
flashcards.back: Aʻoozu billaahi wa qudratihi min sharri maa ajidu wa uhaaziru — 'I seek protection in Allah and His might from the evil of what I feel and fear.'

lessons.title: Du'aa when experiencing fever
lessons.occasion: Recite when one has a fever.
lessons.summary: When we have a fever, we take Allah's great name and seek refuge in Him from harm.

parts.arabic: بِسْمِ اللهِ الْكَبِيْرِ، أَعُوْذُ بِاللهِ الْعَظِيْمِ مِنْ شَرِّ كُلِّ عِرْقٍ نَّعَّارٍ وَّمِنْ شَرِّ حَرِّ النَّارِ
parts.transliteration: Bismillaahil-kabeer, aʻoozu billaahil-ʻazeemi min sharri kulli ʻirqin naʻʻaarin wa min sharri harrin-naar
parts.translation: In the name of Allaah, The Great. I seek refuge with Allaah, The Lofty, from the evil of every spurting vein and from the evil of the heat of the fire.

questions.prompt: The dua for a fever asks Allah for protection from the evil of every spurting vein and from the evil of the heat of the ______.
questions.explanation: We seek refuge from the evil of every spurting vein and the heat of the fire.

flashcards.front: When do we read 'Bismillaahil-kabeer, aʻoozu billaahil-ʻazeem...'?
flashcards.back: When experiencing a fever — seeking refuge in Allah from harm.

lessons.title: Intention to fast (Niyyah)
lessons.occasion: Recite when making the intention to fast.
lessons.summary: We make our intention to fast the next day.

questions.explanation: This is the intention (niyyah) for the next day's fast.

questions.prompt: Fill in the blank: 'Bisawmi ghadin ______' (I am fasting tomorrow).

flashcards.front: Intention to fast — what do we say?
flashcards.back: Bisawmi ghadin nawaytu — 'I am fasting tomorrow.'

lessons.title: Du'aa when breaking fast
lessons.summary: At iftaar we tell Allah we fasted for Him and break our fast with the food He gave us.

parts.arabic: اَللٰهُمَّ لَكَ صُمْتُ وَعَلٰى رِزْقِكَ أَفْطَرْتُ
parts.transliteration: Allaahumma laka sumtu wa ʻalaa rizqika aftartu
parts.translation: O Allaah, I fasted for You and with Your provision (food) do I break my fast.

questions.prompt: Which dua is read when breaking the fast?
options: Allaahumma laka sumtu wa ʻalaa rizqika aftartu
Allaahumma baarik lanaa feehi wa zidnaa minhu
questions.correctAnswer: Allaahumma laka sumtu wa ʻalaa rizqika aftartu
questions.explanation: At iftaar: 'O Allah, I fasted for You and with Your provision I break my fast.'

questions.prompt: Fill in the blank: 'Allaahumma laka ______ wa ʻalaa rizqika aftartu' (O Allah, I fasted for You...).

flashcards.back: Allaahumma laka sumtu wa ʻalaa rizqika aftartu — 'O Allah, I fasted for You and with Your provision I break my fast.'

lessons.title: Du'aa when breaking fast at the home of another
lessons.occasion: Recite for your host when you break your fast at someone's home.
lessons.summary: We make dua for the host: that fasting people break fast at their home, the pious eat their food, and the angels pray for them.

parts.arabic: أَفْطَرَ عِنْدَكُمُ الصَّائِمُوْنَ وَأَكَلَ طَعَامَكُمُ الْأَبْرَارُ وَصَلَّتْ عَلَيْكُمُ الْمَلَائِكَةُ
parts.transliteration: Aftara ʻindakumus-saa-imoona wa akala taʻaamakumul-abraaru wa sallat ʻalaykumul-malaa-ikah
parts.translation: May the fasting persons break their fast at your place and may the pious people partake of your food and may the angels pray for mercy for you.

questions.prompt: When you break your fast at someone's home, who do you make dua will pray for them?
questions.explanation: We ask that the angels pray for mercy for the host.

flashcards.front: Du'a when breaking fast at someone's home — what do we ask for them?
flashcards.back: That fasting people break fast at their home, the pious eat their food, and the angels pray for mercy for them.

lessons.title: Du'aa when wearing a new garment
lessons.occasion: Recite when wearing a new item of clothing.
lessons.summary: We ask Allah for the goodness of the garment and seek protection from its evil.

parts.arabic: اَللٰهُمَّ إِنِّيْ أَسْأَلُكَ مِنْ خَيْرِهِ وَخَيْرِ مَا صُنِعَ لَهُ، وَأَعُوْذُ بِكَ مِنْ شَرِّهِ وَشَرِّ مَا صُنِعَ لَهُ
parts.transliteration: Allaahumma innee as-aluka min khayrihi wa khayri maa suniʻa lah, wa aʻoozu bika min sharrihi wa sharri maa suniʻa lah
parts.translation: O Allaah, I ask You the goodness of this garment and the goodness of that for which it is made. And I seek your protection from the evil of this garment and the evil of that for which it can be used.

questions.prompt: When wearing a new garment we ask Allah for its goodness and seek protection from its ______.
questions.explanation: We ask for the khayr (goodness) and seek protection from the sharr (evil).

flashcards.front: Du'a when wearing new clothes — what do we ask for?
flashcards.back: Allah's goodness in the garment, and protection from its evil (khayr, not sharr).

lessons.title: Du'aa when sighting the new moon
lessons.occasion: Recite when you see the new crescent moon.
lessons.summary: We ask Allah to bring the new month with safety, faith, peace and Islaam.

parts.arabic: اَللٰهُمَّ أَهِلَّهُ عَلَيْنَا بِالْيُمْنِ وَالْإِيْمَانِ وَالسَّلَامَةِ وَالْإِسْلَامِ وَالتَّوْفِيْقِ لِمَا تُحِبُّ وَتَرْضٰى، رَبِّيْ وَرَبُّكَ اللهُ
parts.transliteration: Allaahumma ahillahu ʻalaynaa bil-yumni wal-eemaani was-salaamati wal-islaami wat-tawfeeqi limaa tuhibbu wa tardaa, Rabbee wa Rabbukallaah
parts.translation: O Allaah, bring us the new moon with security and faith, with peace and in Islaam, and in harmony with what our Lord loves and what pleases Him; our Lord and your Lord (O Crescent!) is Allaah.

questions.prompt: The dua for the new moon asks Allah to bring it with security, faith, peace and ______.
questions.explanation: We ask for the new month to come with security, faith, peace and Islaam.

flashcards.front: When do we read 'Allaahumma ahillahu ʻalaynaa bil-yumni wal-eemaan...'?

lessons.title: Du'aa when eating the first fruit of the season
lessons.occasion: Recite when eating the first fruit of a new season.
lessons.summary: We ask Allah to put blessings (barakah) in our fruit, our city, and our weights and measures.

parts.arabic: اَللٰهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِيْ ثَمَرِنَا وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِيْ مَدِيْنَتِنَا وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِيْ صَاعِنَا وَبَارِكْ لَنَا فِيْ مُدِّنَا
parts.transliteration: Allaahumma baarik lanaa fee thamarinaa wa baarik lanaa fee madeenatinaa wa baarik lanaa fee saaʻinaa wa baarik lanaa fee muddinaa
parts.translation: O Allaah, grant blessings in our fruit and grant blessings in our city and grant blessings in our weights and measures.

questions.prompt: When eating the first fruit of the season, we ask Allah for blessings in our fruit, our city, and our ______.
questions.explanation: We ask for barakah in our fruit, our city, and our weights and measures.

flashcards.front: Du'a when eating the first fruit of the season — what do we ask for?
flashcards.back: Barakah (blessing) in our fruit, our city, and our weights and measures.

lessons.title: Du'aa after drinking milk
lessons.summary: After drinking milk we ask Allah to bless it and increase it for us.

parts.arabic: اَللٰهُمَّ بَارِكْ لَنَا فِيْهِ وَزِدْنَا مِنْهُ
parts.transliteration: Allaahumma baarik lanaa feehi wa zidnaa minhu
parts.translation: O Allaah, grant us blessings in it (the milk) and increase (the milk) for us.

questions.prompt: After drinking milk we ask Allah to bless it and to ______ it for us.
questions.explanation: We ask Allah to put blessing in the milk and to increase it for us.

questions.prompt: After drinking milk we ask Allah to bless it and increase it for us.
questions.explanation: Yes — 'O Allah, bless it for us and increase it for us.'

flashcards.back: Allaahumma baarik lanaa feehi wa zidnaa minhu — 'O Allah, bless it for us and increase it for us.'

lessons.title: Du'a revision — match & recall
lessons.occasion: Revision activities covering all Grade 5 duas.
lessons.summary: Match each occasion to its dua and to its meaning.

questions.prompt: Match each occasion to the meaning of its dua:

pairs.right: Open for me the doors of Your mercy
I seek from You Your bounty
I fasted for You and break my fast with Your provision
Bless it and increase it for us

questions.explanation: These are the meanings of four key Grade 5 duas.

questions.prompt: Match each occasion to its dua:
questions.explanation: Recognise each dua by its opening words and when it is read.

flashcards.front: Which two duas are about fasting (Grade 5)?
flashcards.back: Intention to fast (Bisawmi ghadin nawaytu) and breaking the fast (Allaahumma laka sumtu wa ʻalaa rizqika aftartu).
